memes.label: Ambition
memes.description: In A Daughter of Eve, ambition is the driving force that propels characters through the social machinery of Restoration Paris — it elevates some (Rastignac), consumes others (Nathan), and corrupts all who pursue it without limit.

memes.label: Art
memes.description: In Balzac's A Daughter of Eve, art serves as both a genuine vocation and a social performance — Raoul Nathan embodies the tension between creative integrity and the demands of Parisian celebrity, while music represents a lost spiritual purity in the Granville sisters' upbringing.

memes.label: Baron de Nucingen
memes.description: The great Alsatian banker of La Comédie Humaine — a peer of the realm, a handler of millions, and a recurring figure in Balzac's fictional universe. In A Daughter of Eve, Nucingen is resigning his seat in the Chamber of Deputies to du Tillet, while his wife becomes entangled in Marie-Eugénie's financial schemes.

memes.label: Baronne de Nucingen
memes.description: Wife of the great banker Baron de Nucingen and daughter of Père Goriot — in A Daughter of Eve, the Baronne de Nucingen is the financial intermediary who makes the rescue of Raoul Nathan possible, providing forty thousand francs in exchange for Schmucke's bills and Marie-Angélique's personal guarantee.

memes.label: Beauty
memes.description: In *A Daughter of Eve*, beauty is a form of social capital — it opens doors, attracts attention, and creates value, but it is also a trap, binding women to roles defined by male desire rather than personal agency.

memes.label: Bois de Boulogne
memes.description: The great park on the western edge of Paris — in Balzac's time, a fashionable destination for carriage promenades where the aristocracy displayed its wealth, leisure, and social standing.

memes.label: Chamber of Deputies
memes.description: The lower house of the French Parliament under the July Monarchy (1830–1848) — the political arena toward which Raoul Nathan directs his ambitions and the institution that embodies the post-Revolutionary convergence of journalism, finance, and legislative power.

memes.label: Characters
memes.description: All characters appearing in Honoré de Balzac's "A Daughter of Eve" (1839), drawn from the interconnected world of La Comédie Humaine.

memes.label: Charles de Vandenesse
memes.description: Charles de Vandenesse is the elder brother of Comte Félix de Vandenesse, a former ambassador and marquis whose marriage to a de Fontaine and inherited wealth from the Comte de Kergarouët position him among the high aristocracy of Restoration France — a background figure in *A Daughter of Eve* whose marital alliance illuminates the family network within which the novel's central drama unfolds.

memes.label: Claude Vignon
memes.description: A talented critic and writer who appears briefly in A Daughter of Eve as a contributor to Nathan's planned newspaper — a minor role here, but a significant recurring figure across La Comédie Humaine.

memes.label: Comte de Granville
memes.description: The father of Marie-Angélique and Marie-Eugénie, and a distinguished magistrate — Comte de Granville's severe, patriarchal approach to family life shaped both daughters' understanding of marriage and authority, contributing indirectly to their later unhappiness.

memes.label: Comte Félix de Vandenesse
memes.description: The husband of Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse and a distinguished figure in post-Restoration French political life — Vandenesse embodies the well-meaning but emotionally obtuse aristocratic husband whose blindness to his wife's unhappiness drives the novel's central conflict.

memes.label: comtesse-de-granville
memes.description: The pious and severe mother of Marie-Angélique and Marie-Eugénie, whose oppressive religious upbringing shapes both daughters.

memes.label: Comtesse de Montcornet
memes.description: A charming Parisian salon hostess in Balzac's *A Daughter of Eve* (1839) — the Comtesse de Montcornet presided over the most lively and eclectic drawing-room of the Restoration, where Raoul Nathan first met Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse and the novel's central romantic plot was set in motion.

memes.label: Concepts
memes.description: Key themes and concepts explored in Honoré de Balzac's "A Daughter of Eve" (1839).

memes.label: de Marsay
memes.description: A formidable statesman and Prime Minister in Balzac's La Comédie Humaine — de Marsay heads the government during the events of A Daughter of Eve, embodying the cold, calculating political intelligence of the July Monarchy before his death mid-novel precipitates a ministerial crisis.

memes.label: Devotion
memes.description: In *A Daughter of Eve*, devotion is a double-edged virtue — it is Marie-Angélique's inheritance from her religious upbringing, the form her love for Nathan takes, and ultimately the engine of her self-destruction when devotion is given to the wrong object.

memes.label: Dinner at Véry's
memes.description: The midnight supper at Véry's restaurant where Raoul Nathan, Eugène de Rastignac, and Émile Blondet cynically discuss the Comtesse Marie de Vandenesse as a sexual conquest — Rastignac urging the pursuit, Blondet applying mockery, and Nathan waxing poetic about finding his "Beatrice" and "Laura" in a society woman.

memes.label: Ferdinand du Tillet
memes.description: A wealthy Parisian banker and brother-in-law to the protagonist Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse — du Tillet embodies the ruthless financial ambition of post-Restoration France, using debt and political manipulation to destroy Raoul Nathan.

relations.label: Mme. de Montcornet and Mme. d'Espard

memes.label: du Tillet's Financial Scheme
memes.description: Ferdinand du Tillet's financial manipulation — the scheme of speculation, credit manipulation, and legal pressure by which the banker ensnares Raoul Nathan, precipitating the journalist's financial ruin and attempted suicide.

memes.label: Duchesse de Grandlieu
memes.description: The Duchesse de Grandlieu is one of the grandes dames of Restoration Paris whose salon ranks among the most exclusive aristocratic drawing-rooms of the Faubourg Saint-Germain — a gatekeeper of the old nobility whose drawing-room served as a last redoubt of legitimist society during the July Monarchy.

memes.label: Émile Blondet
memes.description: A brilliant but indolent journalist and critic in Balzac's *A Daughter of Eve* — Blondet serves as Raoul Nathan's closest confidant and the novel's resident political sceptic, a man of penetrating judgment who can analyze everyone's affairs except his own.

memes.label: Eugène de Rastignac
memes.description: The Undersecretary of State in de Marsay's government and a rising political figure in Balzac's *A Daughter of Eve* — Rastignac embodies the ambitious social climber navigating between aristocratic salons, financial cabals, and the machinery of power in post-Restoration Paris.

memes.label: Events
memes.description: Key events in Honoré de Balzac's "A Daughter of Eve" (1839), from intimate salon gatherings to political machinations.

memes.label: Félix de Vandenesse's Counterplot
memes.description: Comte Félix de Vandenesse's methodical counter-plot to rescue his wife Marie-Angélique from her affair with Raoul Nathan — a campaign of financial intelligence, political manipulation, and theatrical exposure that restores his conjugal authority without a single overt confrontation.

memes.label: finot
memes.description: A powerful newspaper proprietor and editor in Balzac's Paris — the commercial face of the press.

memes.label: Florine
memes.description: A popular Parisian actress and courtesan — mistress to Raoul Nathan, Florine represents the demi-monde world of theatrical celebrity, financial precarity, and strategic relationships that Balzac uses to mirror the aristocratic society of A Daughter of Eve.

memes.label: florines-apartment
memes.description: The residence of the actress Florine where Nathan lives — a bohemian counterpoint to the aristocratic salons.

memes.label: Genius
memes.description: In Balzac's *A Daughter of Eve*, genius is a double-edged gift — the source of artistic power and the root of personal destruction, embodied most fully in the figure of Raoul Nathan, whose creative brilliance cannot survive the marketplace.

memes.label: Honor
memes.description: The governing principle of masculine conduct in Balzac's Paris — a code of public integrity that often conceals private compromise and serves as both shield and weapon in social competition.

memes.label: Innocence
memes.description: In *A Daughter of Eve*, innocence is the novel's starting condition and its central loss — it is what Marie-Angélique possesses, what protects her, and what she cannot keep once she enters the world of adult passion and social transaction.

memes.label: Italy
memes.description: Italy appears in A Daughter of Eve as the promised destination of romantic escape and, ultimately, as the site of the novel's quiet resolution — a place where travel serves as both a dream of freedom and, later, a return to domestic order.

memes.label: The July Revolution of 1830
memes.description: The July Revolution of 1830 (Trois Glorieuses) — the three-day uprising that overthrew Charles X and the Bourbon Restoration, installing Louis-Philippe and the July Monarchy; the political horizon against which Balzac's characters unknowingly live their dramas of ambition, love, and finance.

memes.label: La Comédie Humaine
memes.description: Honoré de Balzac's monumental cycle of interlinked novels and stories — the fictional universe within which A Daughter of Eve appears as one of the Scènes de la vie privée.

memes.label: Lady Dudley
memes.description: A wealthy English aristocrat resident in Paris, Lady Dudley is one of the grandes dames of Restoration high society — a hostess of grand balls, a sharp-tongued observer of others' affairs, and a figure who embodies the worldly, disillusioned wisdom of experience.

memes.label: lady-dudleys-boudoir
memes.description: The intimate private room at Lady Dudley's residence where Nathan's suicidal despair is discovered and his farewell is spoken.

memes.label: Lady Dudley's Masked Ball
memes.description: The culminating social event of A Daughter of Eve — Lady Dudley's masked ball becomes the stage for the novel's climactic confrontation between Nathan, du Tillet, and the Vandenesse marriage, where hidden identities mirror hidden truths.

memes.label: lord-dudley
memes.description: An elderly English aristocrat who appears at Lady Dudley's ball — a figure from an older, more dissolute generation.

memes.label: Love
memes.description: The central emotional force in Balzac's A Daughter of Eve — a complex interplay of romantic idealism, social ambition, and moral danger in 19th-century Parisian society.

memes.label: Mademoiselle des Touches
memes.description: A celebrated woman writer and salon hostess in Balzac's La Comédie Humaine, Mademoiselle des Touches — who also publishes under the masculine pseudonym Camille Maupin — is one of the most intellectually formidable figures in Restoration Paris, based in part on George Sand and embodying the rare woman who claims artistic independence in a society that grants it only to men.

memes.label: Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse
memes.description: The protagonist of A Daughter of Eve — a young aristocratic wife whose romantic disillusionment drives the novel's central conflict between marital duty and passionate love.

relations.label: Ch. III: The Story of a Happy Woman

memes.label: Marie Confesses to Félix
memes.description: The climactic domestic scene in which Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse, driven by jealousy and her husband's careful orchestration, confesses her emotional infidelity with Raoul Nathan to Comte Félix de Vandenesse — who has already known everything and acted to save her from ruin before she speaks a word.

memes.label: Marie Discovers Florine
memes.description: Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse's gradual and devastating discovery that her idealized lover Raoul Nathan has maintained a long-standing domestic and financial relationship with the actress Florine — a revelation that shatters her romantic illusions and forces her to confront the demi-monde reality beneath Nathan's poetic self-presentation.

memes.label: Marie-Eugénie du Tillet
memes.description: The elder sister of Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse and the wife of the wealthy banker Ferdinand du Tillet — trapped in a cold, controlling marriage, Eugénie serves as a foil to her sister and a warning of what aristocratic marriage can become.

memes.label: marie-returns-to-felix
memes.description: The final reconciliation between Marie-Angélique and Félix de Vandenesse after the crisis of her infatuation with Raoul Nathan.

memes.label: Marquise d'Espard
memes.description: A formidable grande dame of Paris Restoration society, the Marquise d'Espard presides over one of the most exclusive salons of the Faubourg Saint-Germain — sharp-tongued, socially omniscient, and deeply complicit in the romantic entanglement between Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse and Raoul Nathan.

memes.label: marquise-de-listomere
memes.description: An aristocratic salon hostess in Restoration Paris — one of the grandes dames who define the novel's social landscape.

memes.label: marquise-de-vandenesse
memes.description: Marie-Angélique's sister-in-law, who delivers cynical maxims about love and marriage.

memes.label: Marriage
memes.description: The central social institution in A Daughter of Eve — simultaneously an economic contract, a moral obligation, and a constraint upon romantic love in Balzac's Paris.

memes.label: mme-de-manerville
memes.description: A former mistress of Félix de Vandenesse who appears at Lady Dudley's ball and in salon scenes.

memes.label: moina-de-saint-heren
memes.description: A pretty young society woman in Balzac's Paris who voices the allure of forbidden love.

memes.label: Money
memes.description: In *A Daughter of Eve*, money is the hidden infrastructure of all social relationships — it flows through debts, diamonds, and dowries, binding characters together in webs of obligation that no romantic sentiment can dissolve.

memes.label: Mme. de Montcornet's Concert
memes.description: A pivotal salon concert hosted by the Comtesse de Montcornet — the event where Raoul Nathan meets Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse and their fateful relationship begins, setting in motion the central plot of A Daughter of Eve.

memes.label: Nathan's Debt Rescue
memes.description: The clandestine operation mounted by Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse and her sister Marie-Eugénie du Tillet to rescue Raoul Nathan from debtors' prison by raising forty thousand francs through forged bills signed by the old music-master Schmucke and guaranteed by the Baronne de Nucingen.

memes.label: Nathan's Journalism
memes.description: Raoul Nathan's career as a journalist, newspaper founder, and playwright in Restoration Paris — the professional identity that both elevates and undermines him, providing entry to society while ensnaring him in financial obligations that precipitate his downfall.

memes.label: Nathan's Suicide Attempt
memes.description: Raoul Nathan's attempted suicide by charcoal asphyxiation — the crisis point of his financial ruin and romantic entanglement with Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse, occurring between his arrest for debt and his eventual rescue by Marie's intervention.

memes.label: Mme. Octave de Camps
memes.description: Mme. Octave de Camps is a minor but quietly crucial figure in Balzac's *A Daughter of Eve* — an excellent woman whose discretion, loyalty, and protective instinct place her at Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse's side during the two most vulnerable moments of her romantic awakening.

memes.label: Opéra de Paris
memes.description: The great opera house and supreme public theater of Parisian high society in the 1830s — a place of spectacle, rendezvous, and social surveillance where Balzac's characters see and are seen, and where the private dramas of the plot unfold under the gaze of the fashionable world.

memes.label: Organizations
memes.description: Institutions and organizations referenced in Honoré de Balzac's "A Daughter of Eve" (1839).

memes.label: palais-royal
memes.description: The Palais-Royal — commercial and social heart of Restoration Paris, home to Véry's restaurant and a symbol of the city's vibrant public life.

memes.label: Paris
memes.description: The setting and unseen protagonist of A Daughter of Eve — a city whose salons, theaters, and boulevards form the stage on which Balzac's social drama unfolds.

memes.label: Passion
memes.description: The most dangerous form of love in Balzac's moral universe — an overwhelming emotional force that consumes reason, defies social convention, and leads inexorably to ruin.

memes.label: Places
memes.description: Locations in Honoré de Balzac's "A Daughter of Eve" (1839) — the streets, salons, and districts of Restoration-era Paris.

memes.label: Politics
memes.description: In *A Daughter of Eve*, politics is the arena where ambition, finance, and journalism converge — it is the prize that men like du Tillet and Nathan pursue, and the system that Balzac anatomizes with cold precision.

memes.label: quai-conti
memes.description: The Left Bank quay where Schmucke lives — visited by Marie-Angélique during the debt-rescue scheme.

memes.label: Raoul Nathan
memes.description: A talented but struggling writer and journalist in Balzac's *A Daughter of Eve* — Nathan embodies the clash between literary ambition and financial survival in post-Restoration Paris, becoming the object of Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse's romantic fascination.

memes.label: rue-de-clichy
memes.description: The Paris street housing the notorious debtors' prison where Nathan is threatened with imprisonment.

memes.label: rue-de-nevers
memes.description: A street on the Left Bank where Wilhelm Schmucke lives in extreme poverty — the counterpoint to the aristocratic Rue du Rocher.

memes.label: Rue du Mail
memes.description: A Paris street near the Place des Victoires, known in the novel for the Hôtel du Mail — the lodging house where Raoul Nathan was taken after his failed suicide attempt, and where Marie-Angélique's secret correspondence with him was discovered.

memes.label: Rue du Rocher
memes.description: The Parisian residence of the Comte and Comtesse de Vandenesse in the Rue du Rocher — a domestic space that Balzac renders as both a "paradise" of aristocratic refinement and a gilded cage for its dissatisfied mistress, Marie-Angélique.

memes.label: Salon of Mme. de Montcornet
memes.description: The Paris salon of the Comtesse de Montcornet — the most mixed and lively gathering-place of artists, literary men, and financial princes in Balzac's novel, where Raoul Nathan first enters the world of Marie-Angélique de Vandenesse and where the machinery of the plot is set in motion.

memes.label: Schmucke
memes.description: An elderly German musician, former choirmaster to the Margrave of Anspach, and the beloved childhood music-master of Marie-Angélique and Marie-Eugénie — Schmucke is a figure of pure innocence, self-sacrificing devotion, and unworldly goodness whose signature on four bills of exchange enables the rescue of Raoul Nathan and nearly destroys him.

memes.label: Society
memes.description: The all-seeing, all-judging collective force in Balzac's novel — a world of salons, reputations, and unspoken rules that shapes every character's destiny.

memes.label: telemachus
memes.description: Fénelon's didactic novel *Les Aventures de Télémaque* (1699) — a classical reference in the novel's literary culture.

memes.label: The Bourbon Restoration
memes.description: The Bourbon Restoration (1814–1830) — the political regime under which A Daughter of Eve takes place, shaping the novel's social hierarchies, financial dynamics, and the contested position of talent and ambition in a society organized around birth and legitimacy.

memes.label: The Salons of Paris
memes.description: The network of aristocratic and bourgeois drawing-rooms that constituted the social infrastructure of Balzac's Paris — arenas of conversation, reputation management, and covert power.

memes.label: The Two Marriages
memes.description: The paired marriages of Marie-Angélique and Marie-Eugénie de Granville — the foundational event of the novel that sets both sisters on divergent paths through Parisian society.

memes.label: Théâtre Français
memes.description: The state-subsidized national theatre of France, home to the classical repertory — the institution where Raoul Nathan's serious dramatic ambitions met their most humiliating defeat and which symbolizes the tension between literary art and commercial entertainment in Balzac's Paris.

memes.label: Vanity
memes.description: In *A Daughter of Eve*, vanity is the invisible engine of social behavior — it governs ambition, wounds pride, and creates the vulnerabilities through which characters are manipulated, elevated, and destroyed.

memes.label: vergiss-mein-nicht
memes.description: A German song ("Forget-me-not") referenced in the novel — part of the musical and emotional texture of the sisters' childhood.

memes.label: very-restaurant
memes.description: The celebrated Paris restaurant where Nathan, Rastignac, and Blondet share their cynical supper — a moral theater of the novel.

memes.label: Vice
memes.description: In *A Daughter of Eve*, vice is not a matter of individual sin but a structural feature of Parisian society — Balzac depicts a world where every profession, every salon, and every ambition has its characteristic form of moral corruption.
